symptoms.symptom_name: Acute breathlessness / sensation of not being able to catch breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Air-hunger and inability to take a satisfying breath are hallmark hyperventilation symptoms.
Acute dyspnoea is non-specific; PE must be actively screened for and cannot be excluded remotely.
Dyspnoea can be an anginal equivalent, particularly in atypical presentations.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central chest tightness linked to fast breathing, non-radiating, non-exertional

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central, breathing-linked, non-radiating, non-exertional chest tightness is typical of hyperventilation.
Absence of a crushing, radiating, exertional quality lowers ACS probability but does not exclude it without an ECG.
Sympathetic surge in panic produces sinus tachycardia perceived as palpitations.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sense of impending doom, near-syncope and peripheral tingling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Impending doom and paraesthesiae from hypocapnia are characteristic of a panic attack.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear psychological trigger — unguided reading of abnormal blood results

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discrete, acute psychological precipitant immediately preceding symptom onset strongly supports a panic aetiology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild subacute fatigue over recent weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific fatigue is consistent with mild iron deficiency but is not acute or alarming.

symptoms.symptom_name: No melena and no haematemesis (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: symptomatic anaemia from occult GI blood loss
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of overt GI bleeding argues against acute symptomatic blood loss as the cause of the presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lives alone with high health anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Social isolation and health anxiety are predisposing and perpetuating factors for panic attacks.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sinus tachycardia (HR ~110), mild hypertension (BP ~145/90), tachypnoea (RR ~24), SpO2 100%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic tachycardia, transient hypertension and tachypnoea with fully preserved oxygenation fit hyperventilation — but these are clinician-measured values sealed from the AI Doctor, which cannot obtain reliable vitals over chat.
Normal SpO2 does not exclude PE; tachycardia is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiorespiratory examination and soft non-tender abdomen (in-person)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal in-person exam (clear lungs, no murmurs, soft abdomen) supports a benign cause — but this requires a physically-present clinician and is unavailable over chat.

investigation_details.expected_result: no acute ischaemic changes expected given the benign aetiology
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An atypical ACS could be missed if no ECG is obtained during the same-day in-person assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is the key first-line test to screen for ischaemia and is not obtainable via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive tTG-IgA with duodenal biopsy showing intraepithelial lymphocytosis without villous atrophy (Marsh 1); low ferritin

investigation_details.investigation_name: Coeliac serology (tTG-IgA) and duodenal biopsy; iron studies (ferritin)
investigation_details.expected_result: positive tTG-IgA, Marsh 1 histology, low ferritin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive serology with early histological change supports early/asymptomatic coeliac disease — a chronic, non-emergent finding.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line screen for an atypical ACS, which cannot be excluded over chat — mandatory part of the same-day in-person review.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person cardiorespiratory examination and vital signs
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Provides the objective examination and reliable vitals that telehealth chat cannot obtain.
Not indicated as a reflex test given low PE pre-test probability (no pleuritic pain, no DVT features, no risk factors) — reserve for if PE features emerge; routine ordering would fuel health anxiety.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat iron studies and confirmatory gastroscopy (outpatient, via GP/gastroenterology)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms coeliac disease and characterises iron deficiency on a routine outpatient timeline; gastroscopy must precede any gluten-free diet.
